 {"id":"bd-rkql.7","title":"Integrate GATE-FUZZ into CI pipeline with iteration budget tiers","description":"## STATUS: PARTIALLY EXISTS — EXTEND FOR RUST COMPARISON

## Context
The Makefile ALREADY HAS fuzz targets:
- make fuzz-build: builds fuzz harnesses
- make fuzz-smoke: runs 100-iteration smoke test
- make fuzz-run: runs default 1000 iterations
- make fuzz-nightly: runs 100K iterations

These run C-only determinism self-checks. What's MISSING is:
- Rust binary comparison (differential C-vs-Rust)
- GATE-FUZZ as a named CI gate with structured JSONL output
- Iteration budget tiers (smoke/nightly/release) with Rust comparison

## Task
1. Extend existing Makefile fuzz targets to support RUST_FUZZ_BINARY variable:
   - make fuzz-smoke RUST_FUZZ_BINARY=path/to/rust_target
   - Passes --rust-binary flag to fuzz_differential (from bd-rkql.4)

2. Create tools/ci/gate_fuzz.sh wrapping the Makefile targets:
   - Accepts --tier smoke|nightly|release
   - Checks if Rust binary available, skips differential if not
   - Always runs C-only determinism checks (existing behavior)
   - Adds Rust comparison when binary available
   - JSONL logging per harness and aggregate

3. Add GATE-FUZZ to master gate suite (make check-ci already runs fuzz-smoke)

## Acceptance Criteria
- make fuzz-smoke continues to work without Rust binary (existing behavior preserved)
- make fuzz-smoke RUST_FUZZ_BINARY=... adds differential comparison
- gate_fuzz.sh has JSONL output, graceful SKIP when Rust unavailable
- Smoke tier < 60 seconds","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:28:46.426638856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T00:16:06.661486280Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T00:16:06.658921561Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rkql.7","depends_on_id":"bd-rkql","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:28:46.426638856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-rkql.7","depends_on_id":"bd-rkql.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:28:51.957899553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-rkql.8","title":"Standalone fuzz harness sanity tests with crash/UB detection logging","description":"## STATUS: INDEPENDENT TRACK — RUN EXISTING CODE UNDER SANITIZERS

## Context
fuzz_differential.c and fuzz_minimize.c already exist and are complete. This bead
runs them under ASan/UBSan to find memory safety issues or undefined behavior.

This bead is on an INDEPENDENT TRACK — it does not depend on the grammar audit
(rkql.1) or C harness audit (rkql.2). It uses the existing code as-is.

## Task
1. Build fuzz_differential with -fsanitize=address,undefined -fno-omit-frame-pointer
2. Run: fuzz_differential --nightly (100K iterations) under ASan+UBSan
3. Build fuzz_minimize with same sanitizer flags
4. Run: fuzz_minimize --selftest under ASan+UBSan
5. Verify determinism under sanitizers: 10K iterations, same seed, run twice,
   compare all digests — must be bit-identical
6. Create tests/fuzz/test_harness_sanity.sh automating all of the above

## JSONL Log Format
{"ts":"...","step":"asan_fuzz_differential","iterations":100000,"status":"pass","findings":0,"duration_ms":...}
{"ts":"...","step":"asan_fuzz_minimize_selftest","status":"pass","findings":0,"duration_ms":...}
{"ts":"...","step":"determinism_check","iterations":10000,"seed":"fixed","status":"pass","digest_match":true}

## Acceptance Criteria
- 100K iterations under ASan+UBSan, zero findings
- All self-tests pass under sanitizers
- Determinism verified under sanitizers (same seed → same digests)
- test_harness_sanity.sh exists with JSONL output
- If sanitizer finds issues, file them as new beads with P0 priority

## Why Independent Track
Sanitizer runs validate the EXISTING code, not the audited/modified code. Running
them early catches lurking UB that might otherwise be masked. Any findings here
inform the audit work (rkql.2) rather than depending on it.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:30:33.966622688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T20:11:42.267349564Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T20:11:42.266429382Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rkql.8","depends_on_id":"bd-rkql","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:30:33.966622688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-va8n","title":"EPIC: Local Lint and Format Tooling (GATE-CHECK)","description":"## Goal
Install and configure clang-format and clang-tidy locally so that make format-check and
make lint produce real results instead of SKIP.

## Why This Matters
GATE-CHECK (format + lint + strict build) is the first CI blocking gate. Currently these
targets skip locally because the tools are not installed, meaning developers and agents
cannot catch formatting or lint violations before pushing. This creates a feedback gap
where CI rejects commits that looked clean locally.

## What "Done" Looks Like
- clang-format (version matching CI) installed and on PATH
- clang-tidy (version matching CI) installed and on PATH
- make format-check runs and either passes or reports real violations
- make lint runs and either passes or reports real violations
- .clang-format and .clang-tidy configs match CI expectations

## Risk
- Low risk. This is purely toolchain installation.
- Version mismatch between local and CI clang-format could cause spurious diffs","notes":"## Dependency Structure
bd-va8n.1 (clang-format) and bd-va8n.2 (clang-tidy) are independent — both can
be done in parallel. No cross-epic dependencies; this is the lowest-priority
quality-of-life epic.

## CRITICAL: Config Files Do Not Exist
**Neither .clang-format nor .clang-tidy exist in this repository.** These beads
must CREATE the configuration files from scratch, deriving style rules from the
existing codebase patterns. This is more work than simply installing tools —
the style must be audited and codified.

## Rationale
The FEATURE_PARITY_VERIFICATION_20260312.md report (Gap #2) notes that format-check
and lint gates report SKIP locally. The gap is twofold: (1) the tools are not
installed, and (2) the config files do not exist.

## How This Serves Project Goals
This is developer/agent experience improvement, not a feature parity gap per se.
It's P3 because CI already enforces these checks — the gap is in local validation
speed, not correctness.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":105,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:06:36.760207363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:36:57.660348611Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T01:36:57.657555496Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dx","tooling"]}
 {"id":"bd-va8n.1","title":"Create .clang-format config and install clang-format locally","description":"## Context
The FEATURE_PARITY_VERIFICATION report (Gap #2) notes that format-check and lint
gates report SKIP locally because clang-format/clang-tidy are not installed.

**CRITICAL FINDING:** .clang-format DOES NOT EXIST in this repository. It is not
merely missing from the local toolchain — the configuration file itself has never
been created. This bead must CREATE it, not just verify it.

## Background
CI presumably uses a default or inherited clang-format configuration. Without an
explicit .clang-format file in the repository, the formatting style is implicit
and non-reproducible across environments.

## Task
1. **Create .clang-format** in the repository root with a C99-appropriate style:
   - BasedOnStyle: LLVM (or Mozilla — audit existing code to pick the closest match)
   - IndentWidth: 4 (audit existing code for actual indent convention)
   - ColumnLimit: 100 (or whatever the existing code uses — measure before deciding)
   - BreakBeforeBraces, PointerAlignment, etc. — derive from existing codebase patterns
   - Add comments explaining non-obvious choices
2. Install clang-format (version >= 17) on the local build host
3. Run clang-format --dry-run --Werror on ALL .c and .h files
4. Fix any formatting issues, or adjust the .clang-format config so it matches
   the existing code style (config should match code, not the other way around)
5. Verify make format-check passes locally (no longer reports SKIP)

## Style Derivation Methodology
DO NOT blindly pick a style. Instead:
- Sample 10+ representative .c files from src/ and include/
- Run clang-format with different BasedOnStyle values, compare diff sizes
- Pick the style with the smallest diff (most similar to existing code)
- Fine-tune individual options to minimize remaining diffs
- Document which files were sampled and which style was chosen

## Acceptance Criteria
- .clang-format exists in repository root and is committed
- clang-format --version reports >= 17
- make format-check exits 0 (PASS, not SKIP)
- All .c and .h files pass format validation
- Style configuration is derived from existing code, not arbitrarily chosen

## Testing
- Run: clang-format --dry-run --Werror on all src/**/*.c and include/**/*.h
- Run: make format-check
- Verify zero diff between formatted and original code
- Run: clang-format --dump-config to confirm config is parseable

## Why This Matters
Without .clang-format, there is no reproducible formatting standard. Creating it
ensures all agents and developers produce consistently formatted code.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:16:09.030106080Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:36:56.956616559Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T01:36:56.954820040Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-va8n.1","depends_on_id":"bd-va8n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:16:09.030106080Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-va8n.2","title":"Create .clang-tidy config and install clang-tidy locally","description":"## Context
clang-tidy provides static analysis beyond what the compiler warns about. The CI
pipeline runs it as the lint gate, but locally it reports SKIP.

**CRITICAL FINDING:** .clang-tidy DOES NOT EXIST in this repository. It is not
merely missing from the local toolchain — the configuration file itself has never
been created. This bead must CREATE it, not just verify it.

## Background
Without .clang-tidy, clang-tidy runs with default checks, which produces too many
false positives on C99 code. A well-tuned .clang-tidy file enables useful checks
while suppressing irrelevant ones.

## Task
1. **Create .clang-tidy** in the repository root with C99-appropriate checks:
   - Enable: bugprone-*, cert-*, clang-analyzer-*, readability-implicit-bool-conversion
   - Disable: modernize-* (C++ only), cppcoreguidelines-* (C++ only),
     readability-magic-numbers (too noisy for embedded constants),
     bugprone-easily-swappable-parameters (too noisy for callback-heavy API)
   - Configure WarningsAsErrors for critical checks (null-dereference, use-after-free)
   - Add HeaderFilterRegex to only check project headers (not system headers)
   - Document rationale for each enabled/disabled check group
2. Install clang-tidy (version >= 17) on the local build host
3. Run clang-tidy on all source files with the project configuration
4. Triage findings:
   - Fix real bugs immediately
   - Suppress false positives with NOLINT + explanation comment
   - Adjust check configuration to reduce noise without hiding real issues
5. Verify make lint passes locally (no longer reports SKIP)

## Check Selection Methodology
- Start with a conservative set (bugprone-*, cert-*)
- Run on full codebase, categorize all findings
- If a check produces >10 false positives and <2 real bugs, disable it
- Document the triage in the .clang-tidy file as comments

## Acceptance Criteria
- .clang-tidy exists in repository root and is committed
- clang-tidy --version reports >= 17
- make lint exits 0 (PASS, not SKIP)
- Zero un-triaged findings
- Each disabled check has a documented rationale
- NOLINT annotations include explanation (e.g., // NOLINT(bugprone-foo): reason)

## Testing
- Run: clang-tidy src/**/*.c -- -Iinclude/ -std=c99
- Run: make lint
- Verify zero warnings/errors on clean code
- Intentionally introduce a bug (null deref) and verify clang-tidy catches it
- Remove the intentional bug after verification

## Why This Matters
Static analysis catches bugs that tests miss (especially UB, null derefs, type
confusion). Creating .clang-tidy with tuned checks gives meaningful results
instead of a wall of false positives.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:16:20.496825071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:36:57.301315974Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T01:36:57.299330107Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-va8n.2","depends_on_id":"bd-va8n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:16:20.496825071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-yx9r","title":"Reopened full-crate parity closure against /dp/asupersync export surface","description":"The previously closed roadmap mostly covered kernel and selective deferred surfaces, but /dp/asupersync/src/lib.rs still exports many major module families that are not materially present in the ANSI C tree: config, decoding, epoch, evidence, evidence_sink, gen_server, http, io, link, migration, monitor, observability, obligation, record, remote, service, session, spork, transport, types, web, plus feature/platform modules such as cli, database, tls, grpc, messaging, and server. This epic reopens parity planning at the crate level instead of stopping at kernel semantics.","design":"Use /dp/asupersync/README.md, /dp/asupersync/src/lib.rs, the upstream module tree, and the local deferred-surface docs as the primary evidence base. The graph should separate: (1) truth-maintenance tasks that correct the parity narrative, (2) foundation tracks needed before protocol/application stacks, and (3) end-state acceptance/gating work that proves crate-level parity or explicitly records remaining deliberate exclusions. Every implementation track must carry unit tests, integration/conformance fixtures where possible, and logged e2e or smoke artifacts.","acceptance_criteria":"A. The reopened backlog explicitly covers every major Rust export family that remains absent or only stubbed in ANSI C.\nB. The graph distinguishes foundational blockers from higher-surface protocol/application work.\nC. Each child issue stores enough background and verification detail that the original markdown plans are unnecessary.\nD. Final acceptance requires crate-level parity evidence rather than kernel-only success criteria.","notes":"Primary upstream evidence: /dp/asupersync/src/lib.rs, /dp/asupersync/README.md, and upstream module directories such as src/http, src/web, src/grpc, src/service, src/transport, src/distributed, src/database, src/messaging, src/types, src/record, src/obligation, src/lab, src/trace, and src/runtime/reactor. Local evidence that the old closure was too narrow: docs/DEFERRED_SURFACES.md, docs/DEFERRED_SURFACE_REGISTER.md, and docs/SELECTIVE_SURFACE_PORTING_QUEUE.md.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-13T20:38:15.573296808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T22:29:34.427618288Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","core-parity","crate-parity","reopened","upstream-gap"]}
 {"id":"bd-yx9r.1","title":"Rebuild authoritative upstream export-gap inventory and parity truth table","description":"Build a fresh, source-backed gap inventory that compares the Rust crate's exported module surface and README claims against the actual ANSI C public headers, source directories, docs, examples, and tests. This is needed because the prior backlog closure treated kernel parity as if it implied crate parity.","design":"Inventory should enumerate each upstream exported family, classify current ANSI C state as absent, stub-only, partial, or substantive, link the relevant local files if any, and record the concrete user-facing gap. The output should be detailed enough to drive future prioritization without reopening lib.rs.","acceptance_criteria":"A. Every major Rust export family is classified against the ANSI C tree with evidence.\nB. Stub-only or misleadingly named local modules are explicitly called out.\nC. The inventory identifies which higher-surface tracks depend on networking, runtime-reactor, trace/lab, or type-system completion.\nD. The resulting backlog can be audited against this inventory.","notes":"Must cover portable, feature-gated, and platform-specific modules separately. Include examples, CLI, test-helper, and docs surfaces when they materially affect user expectations.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T20:38:16.255862790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T00:46:06.691731999Z","closed_at":"2026-03-14T00:46:06.636636775Z","close_reason":"Completed: refreshed docs/RUST_EXPORTED_SURFACE_INVENTORY.md with a 2026-03-14 crate-vs-C truth table covering portable, feature-gated, platform-gated, and adjacent contract surfaces, including absent/stub-only/partial/substantive classifications and shipped-vs-tree notes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","core-parity","crate-parity","docs","inventory","truth"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yx9r.1","depends_on_id":"bd-yx9r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T20:38:16.255862790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
